summary: Part V formalizes physical realizability as essential surjectivity on pi_0 of the comparison map rho and assembles three cited results (Chen-Gu-Liu-Wen + Else-Nayak, Ogata, Kapustin-Fidkowski) into positive/negative realizability boundaries, a dimension-by-symmetry status table, and three open conjectures. The panel finds the mathematics mostly sound (technical confidence 0.85, 0 critical findings): the CP-versus-general-gapped distinction that anchors the paper is correctly and consistently executed, the two worked propositions (Kitaev-chain Z2 winding, cluster-state string order) are fully hand-verified and reproducibly cross-checked by accompanying Haskell/QuickCheck code that builds and runs exactly (reproducibility 0.84, confidence 0.97), all 39 citations resolve with no fabrications (citation role: minor revision), and two independent context reviewers converged positively (Gemini ACCEPT after three rounds; Codex PASS). Three MAJOR blocking findings require correcting over-claims inside the central theorems, all agreed across the technical and citation roles. (i) Theorem V-B(ii) asserts a 2D interacting-SPT completeness result and a bijection rho -> H^3(G,T) that the cited Ogata paper (arXiv:2101.00426) does not prove; that paper establishes existence/well-definedness of the H^3 index, while completeness for general finite on-site G is an open problem, so labeling it a Theorem violates the paper's own theorem-vs-conjecture discipline. (ii) Theorem V-C conflates the electric Hall conductance sigma_H (the U(1)-charge Kapustin-Fidkowski no-go) with the chiral central charge c_-; these are independent invariants, and the flagship bosonic E8 state (sigma_H=0, c_-=8) is routed through a theorem that provably does not cover it. (iii) Table 1 rows 3-4 inherit both defects. In all three the underlying physics is correct and the weaker true statements survive, so the fixes need no new mathematics; a consolidated 9-item fix list is already dispatched to the authoring worker. Advisory items: a decade-old interacting-fermionic-SPT commuting-projector literature (Gu-Wen, Tarantino-Fidkowski, Wang-Gu) and 2021-2025 work qualifying the Kapustin-Fidkowski wall are uncited and weaken the 'consolidated' framing; src/bordism-realizability/ ships no .cabal build metadata unlike its siblings; the d=1 completeness pointer should cite primary source ogata-spt-chains rather than the ICM survey; and Theorem V-C's proof-mechanism narrative attributes to Kapustin-Fidkowski an adiabatic-curvature argument that belongs to the separately-cited Kapustin-Sopenko. Novelty is modest and honestly disclosed (0.30), concentrated in the genuinely new conjectural profinite-disorder descent criterion (V-3). Because two of the three central theorems overstate what their cited sources establish -- including presenting an open problem as proved -- the panel recommends major revision even though every fix is localized and well-specified.
strengths: The core CP-versus-general-gapped distinction -- im(rho^CP) contained in im(rho), strict where chiral phases exist -- is the paper's principal contribution and is correctly and consistently executed throughout; Corollary cor:proper stands independently of the Theorem V-C attribution defect, since the fermionic electric-Hall (Chern-insulator) witness establishes properness directly from the U(1) Kapustin-Fidkowski theorem.

Strong theorem-vs-conjecture discipline overall: the three open problems are kept in Conjecture environments (V-1, V-2, V-3), never upgraded to theorems, with proved sub-parts correctly separated (e.g. V-2's 'never CP' half = Theorem V-C vs its conjectural 'chirality is the only obstruction' half). The two blocking issues are over-claims inside Theorems, not conjectures mislabeled as theorems.

Two fully self-contained elementary propositions (Kitaev-chain Z2 winding / Majorana number across the (mu,t) plane; cluster-state nonlocal string order with its (cos theta)^m symmetry-breaking degradation) carry complete, hand-verified proofs -- including correction of a previously-flagged anticommutation bug -- and are cross-checked by an accompanying Haskell/QuickCheck stabilizer-and-BdG engine that builds and runs exactly (reproducibility 0.84, confidence 0.97).

Citation integrity is high: all 39 \cite keys resolve to references.bib, the compiled PDF has no unresolved markers, no fabricated or nonexistent reference was found, and eight load-bearing sources were web-verified (Chen-Gu-Liu-Wen, Else-Nayak, Ogata d=1 and d=2, Kapustin-Fidkowski, Walker-Wang, Kitaev Majorana wire, Kapustin-Sopenko).

Conjecture V-3 (realizability over a profinite disorder hull governed by a condensed-cohomological descent obstruction detectable at finite resolution) is a genuinely novel proposal with no located prior art, resting on the series' own condensed/profinite treatment of disorder hulls; it is the one piece carrying real novelty and is honestly marked as conjectural.
Two independent context reviewers converged positively -- Gemini reached ACCEPT after three revision rounds and Codex returned PASS -- consistent with the panel's mostly_sound technical assessment.
weaknesses: [BLOCKING] (thm-VB-2d-completeness-overstated, MAJOR) Theorem V-B(ii) claims Ogata's operator-algebraic index is a COMPLETE invariant in d=2 and that rho is a BIJECTION onto H^3(G,T) ('realized = invariant', 'no two inequivalent models share an index'). The cited paper (arXiv:2101.00426) proves only that 2D on-site-finite-G SPT phases HAVE a well-defined H^3(G,T)-valued index; completeness (equal index implies same phase) for general finite on-site G is open. The weaker true statement -- every H^3 class is realized via V-A surjectivity -- survives; the bijection/completeness claim does not. Fix: keep d=1 as a complete-classification theorem, downgrade d=2 to constructive surjectivity with conjectural completeness. Independently corroborated by the companion Part IV panel (claim TC8).

[BLOCKING] (thm-VC-hall-vs-central-charge-conflation, MAJOR) Theorem V-C writes 'sigma_H != 0 (equivalently c_- != 0)' and routes the bosonic E8 obstruction through Kapustin-Fidkowski. The electric Hall conductance sigma_H (KF's actual result, requiring an on-site U(1) charge symmetry) and the chiral central charge c_- are independent invariants; the flagship E8 state has sigma_H=0 but c_-=8, so the cited KF theorem does not obstruct it. The physics (E8 is not CP-realizable) is correct but requires a distinct chiral-central-charge / thermal-Hall no-go. Fix: separate the two responses, delete the 'equivalently' identification, and re-route the E8 obstruction (theorem statement, Section 5.2, Table 1 row 4) through the c_- result. Corollary cor:proper is unaffected.

[BLOCKING] (table-status-entries, MAJOR) Table 1 inherits both theorem defects: row 3 ('d=2, on-site finite G ... Settled: complete') should read 'constructive; completeness open', and row 4's E8 obstruction should cite a chiral-central-charge / thermal-Hall no-go rather than the electric Kapustin-Fidkowski theorem. This is the paper's headline deliverable, so the corrections must be applied here as well as in the theorem statements.

[ADVISORY] (novelty) A decade-old, mature interacting-fermionic-SPT commuting-projector literature answering exactly the paper's question -- Gu-Wen supercohomology (arXiv:1201.2648), Tarantino-Fidkowski (arXiv:1604.02145, arXiv:1806.09709), Wang-Gu (Phys. Rev. X 10, 031055) -- is uncited. Table 1's two fermionic rows cover only the free-fermion Kitaev chain and Chern insulator and silently omit the interacting case with no scope caveat, weakening the 'consolidated' claim. Add the rows or an explicit scope disclaimer consistent with Remarks VAscope/VBscope.

[ADVISORY] (novelty) 2021-2025 work qualifying how absolute the Kapustin-Fidkowski wall is -- infinite-dimensional-per-site rotor models with nonzero quantized Hall conductance (arXiv:2102.13057 and related), and finite-dimensional generalized-Hall constructions under generalized symmetries (arXiv:2505.20384) -- is not cited. Remark 'nogo' should note the load-bearing finite-dimensional-local-Hilbert-space hypothesis and that the wall is now known to admit near-misses.

[ADVISORY] (reproducibility) src/bordism-realizability/ ships no build metadata (no .cabal, cabal.project, or Makefile), unlike its sibling packages, so a fresh clone running 'cabal build' fails immediately; the paper's 'Computational verification' section gives no build/run command and no GHC or QuickCheck versions, and there is no code URL/DOI (MIT headers present, hence open_source). Add package metadata and a build/run stanza with pinned versions.

[ADVISORY] (citation) Theorem V-B's d=1 completeness is attributed 'proved in' the ICM survey ogata-classification-review (arXiv:2110.04675), which itself points to a separate primary source for that proof. Cite the primary proof ogata-spt-chains (arXiv:2110.04671), already present in references.bib with a bib comment flagging this exact distinction and independently flagged by the Part IV citation review.

[ADVISORY] (citation) Theorem V-C's proof narrative describes an 'adiabatic-curvature two-form is exact' mechanism and grammatically attributes it to Kapustin-Fidkowski, whose actual technique is algebro-geometric (triviality of a vector bundle over (C*)^2, contingent on on-site U(1) symmetry and a thermodynamic limit not stated in the theorem); the adiabatic-curvature account is the separately and correctly cited Kapustin-Sopenko paper's contribution. Reword to separate 'the theorem is Kapustin-Fidkowski's' from 'one way to see why is Kapustin-Sopenko's'.

[ADVISORY] (technical, MINOR) Proposition prop:descent is conditional on Part III's Conjecture III-2 (invoked only mid-proof) yet sits in an unconditional Proposition environment; label it conditional in the statement to keep the theorem-vs-conjecture boundary crisp.

[ADVISORY] (citation, MINOR) The twice-stated claim that chiral phases are gapped-realizable via coupled-wire/network constructions carries no citation, and the toric code and Levin-Wen string-nets are named as commuting-projector-realized examples without citation (illustrative asides, but inconsistent with the paper's otherwise careful citation discipline).
questions: For Theorem V-B(ii): do you intend to downgrade the d=2 result to constructive surjectivity with conjectural completeness, or can you supply a completeness proof for 2D interacting on-site-finite-G SPT phases (currently an open problem)?
For the E8 obstruction (sigma_H=0, c_-=8): which specific chiral-central-charge / thermal-Hall commuting-projector no-go will you cite in place of the electric Kapustin-Fidkowski theorem?

Will the 'consolidated' status table gain interacting-fermionic-SPT rows (Gu-Wen / Tarantino-Fidkowski / Wang-Gu), or an explicit scope caveat matching the paper's practice in Remarks VAscope and VBscope?

Will src/bordism-realizability/ ship a .cabal/cabal.project like its sibling packages, and will the 'Computational verification' section state the build/run command and the GHC and QuickCheck versions used to produce the quoted numbers?
